title: TEEN PARENT SUCCESS PROGRAM - 211info
text: OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION TEEN PARENT SUCCESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides equal access to school programs and extracurricular activities to students who might be, are, or have been pregnant. Schools are required to treat pregnant and parenting students the same way they treat other students who are similarly able or unable to participate in school activities. Facilitates an environment where teenage parents can complete their education in a comprehensive and supportive school environment. On-site child care is available at some schools. 255 Capitol Street NE Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Pregnant or parenting teens enrolled in participating high schools Hours: Monday-Friday during school hours Email (541) 510-5704 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 947-5600 Main phone Main phone (541) 510-5704 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for information FEES: None for Teen Parenting Program. For child care, minimal fees may apply (varies by school). DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: School Based Teen Parent/Pregnant Teen Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER ASSISTANCE AND PROTECTION - OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - OREGON SCHOOL DIRECTORY EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - MCKINNEY-VENTO HOMELESS EDUCATION ASSISTANCE EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM FOOD - CHILD AND ADULT CARE FOOD PROGRAM FOOD - OREGON STATE SUMMER FOOD SERVICE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
